pi_items.source: Explicitly defined in task description and checked in export_result.sh.
Explicitly defined in task description and checked in export_result.sh.
Explicitly defined in the synthetic specification document created by setup_task.sh.
Explicitly defined in the synthetic specification document created by setup_task.sh.
Calculated using the provided formula in the synthetic spec (Radial Amplitude 0.5 km = SMA * delta_ECC). 0.5 / 6892.14 ≈ 0.0000725464, rounded to 0.0000725 in metadata.

Calculated using the provided formula in the synthetic spec (Cross-track Amplitude 2.5 km = SMA * sin(INC) * delta_RAAN_radians). delta_RAAN_deg = 2.5 / (6892.14 * sin(97.4°)) * (180/pi) ≈ 0.0209575. 45.0 + 0.0209575 ≈ 45.02096.
Explicitly used as the tolerance limit for SMA matching in the verifier.py logic.
Explicitly used as the tolerance limit for INC matching in the verifier.py logic.
Explicitly used as the tolerance limit for ECC matching in the verifier.py logic.
Explicitly used as the tolerance limit for RAAN matching in the verifier.py logic.
Explicitly enforced as the maximum acceptable drift distance limit in the verifier.py physical stability check.
Explicitly enforced as the minimum acceptable drift distance limit in the verifier.py physical stability check.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically generated mission specifications injected via setup_task.sh. The target orbital parameters for the Deputy satellite are calculated directly from the provided formulas: an expected eccentricity of ~0.0000725 (derived from a 0.5 km radial baseline) and an expected RAAN of ~45.02096 (derived from a 2.5 km cross-track baseline). All tolerance boundaries (SMA, INC, ECC, RAAN) and physical drift limits (max 3.0 km, min 0.4 km) perfectly match the enforced rules in the verifier scripts.
